title: PRD: PM Operating Rhythm Automation
problem_summary: PMs lose high-value time to recurring reporting and follow-through work.
outcome_summary: Reduce the time needed to produce high-quality status and coordination outputs.
scope_summary: Automate bi-weekly status mail, issue log maintenance, meeting records, and next-step extraction.
strategic_context_summary: The PRD should preserve the challenger posture: a trusted PM operating layer that improves recurring workflow continuity without claiming broad PM-platform replacement.
value_hypothesis: If ProductOS keeps the PM workflow spine traceable, teams should get better draft quality and faster handoff with less reconstruction work.
target_outcomes: Increase trust in automated PM coordination outputs.

prioritization.priority_rationale: The PRD is the execution handoff that translates the operating-rhythm problem into bounded delivery work.
prioritization.reviewer_handoff: Design and engineering should review scope boundaries before story and acceptance automation proceeds.

scope_boundaries: Stay within recurring PM coordination outputs and adjacent workflow state maintenance.
Preserve explicit PM review gates before any broader launch or support claims.
out_of_scope: Replacing all incumbent planning and execution systems.
General-purpose PM automation beyond the evidence-backed operating-rhythm wedge.
open_questions: Which source coverage threshold should block draft generation entirely versus trigger a PM review request?
handoff_risks: Story generation may drift if the PRD stops carrying the persona and prioritization spine.
Teams may over-claim automation breadth if the scope boundary is not preserved in delivery handoff.
